title: EngineeringChangeRequest
description: An ECR is created when an IncidentReport matures into a change (IRB disposition 'update as ECR'). It carries the change through Change Analysis: the Risk Agent sets the track, and the boards (TRB technical / CRB business) decide. The business decision is made on cost, person-hour, lead time, and effectivity. An approved ECR evolves into an EngineeringChangeNotice (ECN).

incidentId.description: The IncidentReport this ECR matured from (IRB disposition 'update as ECR').

createdAt.description: ISO-8601 UTC timestamp the ECR was opened.

title.description: Short headline for the requested change.

description.description: What the change is and why, carried forward from the incident root-cause analysis.

requestedBy.description: The principal that owns the ECR (typically the Change Specialist).

affectedItems.description: Configuration items / BOM lines implicated by the change. Drives supplier impact analysis.

complianceClass.description: Regulated-industry compliance class; biases the Risk Agent toward Full Track.

disposition.description: CM change-disposition of the pre-change product (severity classification).

track.description: Fast Track bypasses the CRB (Change Specialist / Risk Agent decision); Full Track carries a business decision and goes to CRB then CIB.

riskScore.description: Risk Agent composite score driving the Fast/Full Track gateway.

businessImpact.description: The four CRB decision dimensions (Change Analysis / Supplier Business Analysis).

effectivity.description: Planned date the change takes effect.

boardDecisions.description: Decisions recorded as the ECR passes the boards (TRB technical, CRB business). HITL via Action Center.

decision.description: TRB: closed/updated. CRB (supplier business analysis): approved/rejected/feedback-for-change.

aiAssisted.description: True if an AI briefing pack pre-rendered this decision.

ecnId.description: Set once the ECR evolves into an ECN.
